raw_text:
All three significant lines are well-evidenced. The broad searches have returned consistent, reconciling results across all sources — no narrow follow-up queries are needed. I'll now compose the full commentary.

---

# MARKETING DEPARTMENT — VARIANCE COMMENTARY
**Period: February 2026**

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The marketing department recorded total spend of $117,100 against a budget of $93,000, producing an adverse variance of $24,100 (+25.9%) for the month.

The overrun is driven almost entirely by a single strategic decision: the deliberate pull-forward of the Q2 spring digital campaign into February, approved by the CMO and noted by the CFO for reforecast. This action brought the full Q1/Q2 digital budget into February, inflating Digital Advertising by $28,500 against plan. This is a timing variance rather than a structural cost increase; March and April digital budgets are expected to be zeroed accordingly in the revised forecast.

Partially offsetting the digital overrun, Events & Conferences came in $5,500 below budget following the postponement of the Q1 Sales Summit to April due to venue availability. A venue credit has been secured and the cost is expected to reclassify to April.

Content & Creative was modestly over budget by $1,200, reflecting a one-off strategy workshop fee from new agency Vivid Studio billed in arrears into February.

Finance should confirm that the revised digital forecast for March and April reflects the pull-forward, and that the April events budget is updated to absorb the rescheduled Sales Summit cost.

---

## LINE COMMENTARY

---

**Digital Advertising | +$28,500 (+57.0%) | Budget: $50,000 | Actual: $78,500**

Digital Advertising is over budget by $28,500 (+57.0%), representing the single largest variance in the period and the primary driver of the full-report overrun.

The variance is fully explained by a pre-approved strategic decision to pull the Q2 spring campaign forward from its original March launch window into February. Per Slack communications dated 28 January 2026, the CMO approved the accelerated timeline that same morning, with the agency briefed on 28 January and the campaign going live on 5 February. A formal CMO approval email confirmed that the full Q2 digital budget of $78,500 was to be allocated to February, with March and April digital budgets to be zeroed in the revised forecast. The CFO was noted on the reforecast requirement.

This is a timing variance: spend has been brought forward, not created in addition to plan. The actual spend of $78,500 reconciles precisely to the approved figure cited in the CMO email.

**Action required:** Finance to confirm March and April digital budgets are zeroed in the revised forecast to prevent a double-count.

Sources:
- Slack - 2026-01-28 - slack-digital_advertising-broad - "Confirmed — moving the Q2 spring campaign to February launch. Agency recommends 28 Jan brief to 5 Feb launch window. This pulls the full March digital budget into February. CMO approved this morning."
- Gmail - 2026-01-28 - gmail-digital_advertising-broad - "CMO email approving campaign pull-forward. Full Q2 digital budget ($78,500) allocated to February. March and April digital budgets to be zeroed in revised forecast. CFO noted for reforecast."
- Calendar - 2026-02-05 - calendar-digital_advertising-broad - "Spring campaign launch — paid search and social activated 09:00. Agency briefed 28 Jan per CMO decision. All creatives approved 4 Feb."

---

**Events & Conferences | -$5,500 (-27.5%) | Budget: $20,000 | Actual: $14,500**

Events & Conferences is under budget by $5,500 (-27.5%), representing a favourable timing variance.

The underspend is fully explained by the postponement of the Q1 Sales Summit, which had been budgeted for February. On 15 January, the events team confirmed via both Slack and email that the venue was unavailable for the February slot, and the summit was rescheduled to 18 April. No cash outflow occurred for this event in February; a venue credit of $5,500 has been secured. The $14,500 of actual spend relates to other planned events and conferences that proceeded as expected.

This saving is temporary: the $5,500 venue credit is expected to flow through the April budget when the rescheduled Sales Summit takes place.

**Action required:** Finance to ensure April Events & Conferences budget is uplifted by $5,500 to accommodate the rescheduled summit.

Sources:
- Slack - 2026-01-15 - slack-events_&_conferences-broad - "UPDATE on Q1 Sales Summit — moving to April due to venue availability issues. February slot cancelled. Budget savings of $5,500 will carry forward to April event. No refunds needed — venue credit held."
- Gmail - 2026-01-15 - gmail-events_&_conferences-broad - "Events team email confirming Q1 Sales Summit moved to April. Venue credit of $5,500 secured. No cash outflow in February for this item. Savings tracked against April budget."
- Calendar - 2026-04-18 - calendar-events_&_conferences-broad - "Q1 Sales Summit rescheduled — now 18 April. Previous February booking cancelled 15 January."

---

**Content & Creative | +$1,200 (+15.0%) | Budget: $8,000 | Actual: $9,200**

Content & Creative is over budget by $1,200 (+15.0%), reflecting a one-off billing timing item.

The variance is fully explained by a strategy workshop fee charged in arrears by new agency Vivid Studio. Per Slack communications dated 20 January 2026, Vivid Studio was onboarded in January and delivered campaign assets ahead of schedule, with the understanding that January strategy work of $1,200 would be billed into the February invoice. This was noted as CMO-approved. The formal invoice, received 1 February, confirms the February billing of $8,000 (standard retainer) plus $1,200 (January strategy workshop), totalling $9,200 — reconciling precisely to actuals.

This is a one-off timing item. The February retainer itself is on budget; the overage is attributable solely to the catch-up billing of January strategy work.

Sources:
- Slack - 2026-01-20 - slack-content_&_creative-broad - "New creative agency (Vivid Studio) briefed in January. Campaign assets delivered ahead of schedule — February billing will include January strategy work ($1,200 extra). Approved by CMO."
- Gmail - 2026-02-01 - gmail-content_&_creative-broad - "Invoice from Vivid Studio — February retainer $8,000 + January strategy workshop $1,200 = $9,200 total. CMO approved. Strategy work delivered 28 January."

---

## INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES

The following line items recorded variances below the significance threshold and were not subject to detailed investigation:

- **Marketing Software** — $100 adverse (+2.0%). Immaterial. No investigation required.
- **PR & Comms** — $200 favourable (-2.0%). Immaterial. No investigation required.
